articles.headline: Leite leads Portland against Las Vegas after 20-point game

articles.headline: Dream face the Storm on 5-game skid
articles.description: Seattle Storm (6-17, 1-12 Western Conference) at Atlanta Dream (12-9, 7-3 Eastern Conference)

articles.headline: Sydney Taylor scores 10 of her 16 points in the 3rd quarter and the Sky beat the Mercury 77-66
articles.description: — Sydney Taylor scored 10 of her 16 points in the third quarter, Natasha Cloud finished with 11 points and six assists, and the Chicago Sky beat the Phoenix Mercury 77-66 on Tuesday night.

articles.headline: Jessica Shepard secures 3rd triple-double of the season and the Wings beat the Liberty 88-77
articles.description: — Jessica Shepard had 22 points, 12 rebounds and 11 assists for her third triple-double of the season, and the Dallas Wings beat the New York Liberty 88-77 on Tuesday night.

images.name: Jessica Shepard gets her 3rd triple-double of the season
images.caption: Jessica Shepard gets her 3rd triple-double of the season

articles.headline: Who has the most triple-doubles in WNBA history? Key stats
articles.description: A triple-double is an impressive feat. Which WNBA players have achieved it? Check out a historical rundown of stat leaders.

images.name: Alyssa Thomas [608x342]
images.caption: Alyssa Thomas holds the WNBA record for career triple-doubles.

articles.headline: Sparks' Nneka Ogwumike headlines WNBA All-Star Game reserves
articles.description: Los Angeles Sparks forward Nneka Ogwumike headlines the 12 reserves selected by league coaches Tuesday for the WNBA All-Star Game on July 25 in Chicago.

images.name: Nneka Ogwumike [600x400]
images.caption: Over 440 career WNBA games, Nneka Ogwumike has averaged 16.8 points and 7.5 rebounds and shot 54% from the field.

articles.headline: Fever's Clark hopeful to play Wed., says back-to-back unlikely
articles.description: Fever star Caitlin Clark, who has spent the past two weeks rehabbing from a back injury, said she is "very hopeful" to play Wednesday against the Sparks but admitted it would be difficult for her to play the next day against the Mercury.

images.name: Caitlin Clark [600x400]
images.credit: Photo by Pepper Robinson/NBAE via Getty Images

articles.headline: Guard Chennedy Carter waived by champion Las Vegas Aces
articles.description: Guard Chennedy Carter, the No. 4 pick by the Atlanta Dream in the 2020 draft out of Texas A&M, has been waived by the Aces, the team announced Tuesday. She played in 13 of Las Vegas' 21 games, averaging 12.2 points.

articles.headline: Geno Auriemma says hard fouls on Caitlin Clark not a 'referendum on America'
articles.description: Geno Auriemma says on-court confrontations involving Caitlin Clark should not be viewed as a "referendum on America."

images.name: Caitlin Clark lands hard, takes hand to the neck
images.caption: Caitlin Clark lands hard, takes hand to the neck

articles.headline: How to watch Robin Roberts, Geno Auriemma WNBA broadcast
articles.description: Robin Roberts and Geno Auriemma turn back the clock by broadcasting Wings vs. Liberty, 30 years after calling ESPN's first WNBA game. Here's how to catch the broadcast.

articles.headline: WNBA Power Rankings: Valkyries ride 5-win streak to No. 1
articles.description: Golden State's defense and depth are paying dividends as the top spot once again changes hands.

images.name: Gabby Williams, Veronica Burton [1296x729]
images.caption: Gabby Williams (No. 1) leads the Valkyries in scoring while Veronica Burton (No. 22) leads them in assists at 5.4 per game.

images.name: Washington Mystics vs. Golden State Valkyries - Game Highlights
images.caption: Watch the Game Highlights from Washington Mystics vs. Golden State Valkyries, 07/06/2026

article.headline: Portland Fire roll past Toronto Tempo 99-80 in first matchup between WNBA expansion teams
article.description: — Emily Engstler had 16 points and seven rebounds, Portland dominated the second half, and the Fire defeated the Toronto Tempo 99-80 on Saturday in the inaugural matchup between these two expansion teams.
article.linkText: Portland Fire roll past Toronto Tempo 99-80 in first matchup between WNBA expansion teams

images.name: Lexi Held nails it from behind the arc
images.caption: Lexi Held nails it from behind the arc, 05/23/2026

video.headline: Lexi Held nails it from behind the arc

video.description: Lexi Held nails it from behind the arc, 05/23/2026
video.shortDescription: Lexi Held nails it from behind the arc, 05/23/2026

tracking.trackingName: WNBA_One-Play (Lexi Held nails it from behind the arc) 2026/05/23 ESHEET

article.story: TORONTO -- — Emily Engstler had 16 points and seven rebounds, Portland dominated the second half, and the Fire defeated the Toronto Tempo 99-80 on Saturday in the inaugural matchup between these two expansion teams. The Fire led 66-61 through three quarters and a 3-pointer from Nyadiew Puoch capped an 11-0 run early in the fourth that left Portland in command, 77-63. The Fire (3-3) scored 33 points in the fourth quarter and came up just short of the first 100-point game in their inaugural season. Portland outscored Toronto 55-36 in the second half. Carla Leite had 15 points and nine assists for Portland, Bridget Carleton added 15 points, Megan Gustafson had 14 and Sarah Ashlee Barker scored 12 for the Fire. Engstler blocked four shots. Marina Mabrey had 19 points and eight assists for Toronto (3-4) and rookie Kiki Rice matched her season high with 19 points on 8-of-11 shooting. Brittney Sykes had 10 points. There were 10 lead changes in the first 15 minutes of play, the last coming on a 3-pointer by Mabrey that started a 9-0 run by the Tempo for a 39-30 lead. After a timeout, Portland rallied and eventually took a 42-40 lead with about a minute left in the half. It was 44-all at halftime. Mabrey had 15 points and seven assists in the first half. Nyara Sabally returned from a two-game absence with a neck injury for Toronto. She scored nine points in 23 minutes of playing time. Portland's Karlie Samuelson, who has been out with a left foot injury, was listed as available but did not make her season debut. Up next Portland: at New York on Monday. Toronto: at Chicago on Wednesday. ------ AP WNBA: https://apnews.com/hub/wnba-basketball
